QF.id: VB Fund Distribution and Channel Management_30

question.text: One of the thresholds triggering mandatory commission disclosure by a mutual fund distributor is when the total commission received across all AMCs in the industry exceeds __________ per annum.

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Distributors must disclose commission details if ANY of the following thresholds are met: (i) presence in more than 20 locations, (ii) AUM exceeds Rs. 100 crore, (iii) total commission received across all AMCs exceeds Rs. 1 crore per annum, or (iv) commission received from a single AMC exceeds Rs. 50 lakh per annum.
**The Trap:** Rs. 50 lakh is the threshold for commission from a SINGLE AMC, not the industry-wide threshold. The question specifically asks about the industry-wide (across all AMCs) threshold, which is Rs. 1 crore. Confusing these two thresholds is a common exam trap.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Commission disclosure requirements are important in NISM V-B. All four thresholds should be memorized.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Industry-wide commission threshold = Rs. 1 crore. Single AMC commission threshold = Rs. 50 lakh. Meeting ANY one threshold triggers the disclosure requirement.
